description_long: This is a roughly triangular fragment of stone, featuring a reddish-brown base color with distinct, angular yellowish bands running diagonally across one face. The stone appears to be a broken shard, with jagged and uneven edges, and a relatively flat, broad surface on one side. The reverse side is mostly plain, showing the natural, unadorned stone texture with some minor pitting and discoloration. The thickness of the object is modest, and the overall form suggests it may have once been part of a larger decorative or functional stone element. The surface is matte and rough, with visible grain and weathering marks, indicating exposure to the elements. The colored bands suggest intentional human modification, possibly from an architectural or ornamental context.
description: A triangular, reddish-brown stone shard with angular yellow bands on one face and rough, weathered edges.
description_view_0: The broadest face is visible, showing reddish-brown stone with three distinct yellow diagonal bands and rough, jagged edges.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the stone's thin profile and uneven, chipped edge, with little surface detail visible.
description_view_2: The reverse side is mostly plain, showing the natural stone surface with some pitting and no visible banding.
description_view_3: Another angled side view highlights the thickness and the transition between the decorated and plain surfaces.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a fragment in a decorative barrier
attribute_pattern: Angular, parallel yellow bands on one face, contrasting with the natural stone color; the pattern is geometric and likely artificial.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte with visible pitting and minor discoloration, indicating moderate weathering and exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; not embedded or covered by vegetation.
